name: smc-chart-analysis
description: Smart Money Concepts (SMC) and ICT-style chart analysis skill. Analyzes any market with one command - fetches real data, runs multi-timeframe analysis, identifies trade setups with entry/stop/targets. Use when user asks to "analyze [SYMBOL]", wants SMC/ICT analysis, asks about liquidity sweeps, order blocks, fair value gaps, or market structure, or wants trade setup recommendations.
capabilities: Determines market bias from higher timeframe structure
Identifies liquidity levels and recent sweeps
Locates order blocks and fair value gaps
Constructs trade setups with entry, stop, and targets
Provides risk assessment with R:R ratios
author: AaronAbuUsama
securitySummary: Documentation-only skill containing no executable code, no file system access, no network calls, and no external commands. All 271 static findings are false positives triggered by markdown code examples and trading terminology misinterpreted as security patterns.
valueStatement: Traders need systematic methods to interpret market structure and identify institutional trading patterns. This skill provides AI-powered analysis using SMC/ICT methodology to identify bias, entry zones, stop levels, and targets.
userTitle: Analyze markets with Smart Money Concepts
limitations: Does not execute trades or connect to exchanges
Requires external data source for mechanical analysis
Does not provide financial advice or guarantees
Analysis quality depends on input data accuracy

useCases.title: Learn SMC methodology
useCases.description: Understand market structure, liquidity concepts, and order flow patterns through guided analysis examples.

useCases.title: Daily market analysis
useCases.description: Get structured breakdowns of bias, key levels, and trade setups for specific symbols and timeframes.

useCases.title: Strategy framework
useCases.description: Use SMC detection patterns and narrative templates to build systematic trading approaches.

promptTemplates.title: Basic analysis
promptTemplates.prompt: Analyze [SYMBOL] on the [TIMEFRAME] timeframe using SMC methodology. Identify bias, key liquidity levels, order blocks, and any trade setups.

promptTemplates.title: Setup focus
promptTemplates.prompt: What is the current setup grade for [SYMBOL]? Include entry zone, stop loss, and targets with R:R ratios.

promptTemplates.title: Liquidity focus
promptTemplates.prompt: Map all unswept liquidity for [SYMBOL]. Where are BSL and SSL levels? What recent sweeps occurred?

promptTemplates.title: Scenario planning
promptTemplates.prompt: Present bullish and bearish scenarios for [SYMBOL]. What triggers each scenario? What are the confluence factors and targets?

bestPractices: Always present analysis as scenarios, not predictions
Include risk warnings with every setup
Grade setups honestly - do not oversell low-quality setups
antiPatterns: Making definitive price predictions or guaranteed outcomes
Ignoring contradictory signals in the analysis
Providing trading advice instead of educational analysis

faq.question: What markets does this skill analyze?
faq.answer: Any market with price data including crypto, forex, stocks, and futures. Supports symbols like BTC/USDT, ETH/USDT, and SOL/USDT.

faq.question: What timeframes are supported?
faq.answer: From 1-minute to weekly charts. Multi-timeframe analysis combines higher timeframe bias with lower timeframe entry timing.

faq.question: Does this skill execute trades?
faq.answer: No. This is an analysis tool only. It provides educational market breakdowns and does not connect to any exchange or broker.

faq.question: Is my trading data safe?
faq.answer: Yes. This skill only processes symbol and timeframe inputs you provide. No personal data or trade history is collected or stored.

faq.question: What if the analysis seems wrong?
faq.answer: Always validate AI analysis with your own research. This skill provides educational frameworks, not financial advice or guaranteed predictions.

faq.question: How is this different from trading indicators?
faq.answer: This skill provides AI interpretation of market patterns rather than mechanical signals. It explains the reasoning behind bias and setups.
